title: Monday vibe update: live trials, multi-board support, item views & AI
excerpt:
Hi everyone, 
I’m Amichay Even Chen, the Product Manager leading monday vibe :waving_hand:t2: 
Really glad to share what’s coming next and what we’ve been rolling out, shaped by community feedback. 
monday vibe - Jan 202…

posts.author: amichay (Amichay Even Chen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

I’m Amichay Even Chen, the Product Manager leading monday vibe

Really glad to share what’s coming next and what we’ve been rolling out, shaped by community feedback.

monday vibe - Jan 2026 edition:

  • 7-day live app trial: publish and share vibe apps with other users in your account before purchasing a vibe package, and see how they perform in a real workflow.

image1920×992 242 KB

  • Full-page apps with multi-board support: create a full-page app (left pane) and connect it to up to 5 boards for more complex workflows

Connect up to 5 different boards

  • Share published app with URL: create a dedicated link you can share with account members to view your app in a full screen - make you vibe feel like a dedicated application!

image960×562 58.8 KB

  • Item page apps: build custom item views with monday vibe. These views are added to the board and display data from any selected item.

image1042×746 54.5 KB

image2398×1634 463 KB

  • AI-powered apps: build apps that use AI for summarizing, translating, searching, and more.

image2330×1650 422 KB

Just ask the chat to add AI or websearch to your app!

  • Mobile vibe apps: Mobile apps now have improved support, featuring an easy-to-use QR code from the mobile preview.

image1920×1162 190 KB

On Android phones, you can even save a shortcut directly to the vibe app on your phone’s home screen.

Exclusive for our community: if these features aren’t available in your account yet, you can request early access here: https://wkf.ms/45donSd

Happy to answer any question! Cheers to 2026

posts.author: WirkKarl (Karl)
posts.cookedHtml: Nice update, thanks for sharing. The 7-day live trial sounds especially useful since it lets teams test vibe apps in real workflows before committing. Looking forward to seeing how multi-board support and item views improve day-to-day usage.

posts.author: MelH (Melanie)
posts.cookedHtml:
Feedback from trying it out today and watching webinars:

  • On the two webinars it showed Chart visualisations but any attempt to use vibe I couldn’t get to something similar so its not clear how you get to that from the webinar

  • training really doesn’t seem clear on how to use Vibe to get to anything like the demos in webinars and promo pages (the chart visuals I mention above, plus also there are videos showing how you can create a vibe app that replaces the Home login screen)

  • Not clear on pricing structure - is the price per month for 10 apps per user or on top of what you pay - 100 users at the publicised $90 a month is a lot if its $90 per user. Also really not clear on what would use AI credits - when I asked it (like was suggested on some guidance on the Monday.com vibe promo page) it said it wouldn’t need credits as it used no AI functionality, yet elsewhere on the guidance about Vibe, it said any update to a Vibe created dashboard (say someone updated an item or a column value) it said that would use credits.

  • None of the webinars really make it clear how it views for individual users - say for instance an Approvals app. If we have senior Execs that need to approve things in different workflows on different boards - say Sales transaction, a HR approval to recruit, a confirmation on a campaign because the spend threshold is at Exec level, we would really want an Approvals app that shows all the approvals needed across the org by that person - or do they have to go find each board? Otherwise its easier to do a makeshift dashboard connected to all boards that have an approvals workflow.

  • In preview and even in show within board, it seems so many things aren’t clickable to drill down to the data - which makes it like PowerBI. The benefit of dashboards is that you can easily click through from charts or tables to get to the full data/activities/tasks. Or would this be different once it was published?

  • Its not clear on the 7 day trial is that just one 7 day trial for one app, or do you get 7 days per app?

    Definitely need more webinars and live trainings please so we can ask more questions, as meetings with Account Managers focus more on sales push.

    (I’m an Enterprise Admin for both Work Management and CRM)
